text:
<think>First, I should acknowledge that while no city is "literally" cloudy 100% of the time, there are cities famous for persistent cloudiness.</think>
No city is truly cloudy all the time, but some are well-known for having very frequent overcast weather.
<think>I should provide a specific example of such a city, ideally one with meteorological data supporting its reputation.</think>
Tórshavn, the capital of the Faroe Islands, is often cited as one of the cloudiest cities in the world.
<think>It would be helpful to include some data or a comparison to support this claim.</think>
On average, Tórshavn sees over 840 hours of sunshine per year, which means it is cloudy or overcast for much of the time.
<think>I should clarify that other cities are also very cloudy, perhaps mentioning one or two for context.</think>
Other notably cloudy cities include Reykjavík in Iceland and Seattle in the United States.
<think>Finally, I can reiterate that while these cities are extremely cloudy, "literally all the time" is an exaggeration.</think>
While these places have persistently cloudy skies, there are still occasional breaks of sunshine.
